final_output.final_deliverable:
Report of attempt to follow links from a climate-change news article to (1) a scientific research paper or study, (2) a government policy document, and (3) an organization's position statement

1) Mandate
- Start at a news article about climate change (find one via search). From that article, follow links to find: (1) a scientific research paper or study, (2) a government policy document, and (3) an organization's position statement. For each, provide the URL, a summary of the content, and explain how it relates to the original article. Document your exploration path.

2) Summary of what was done (high-level)
- I ran a targeted search to find a recent mainstream news article about climate change that would likely include outbound links to primary sources (scientific studies, government policy documents, organization position statements). Several candidate news articles and URLs were discovered by the search (listed below). [These candidate article titles and URLs come from the search results produced in the search node; see Section 4, "Search results (candidate news articles)" for the list and URLs].
- I then attempted to visit the selected news article and to follow outbound links. The browsing attempts did not load a news article; instead the visits returned Wikipedia special pages. The only pages actually visited and for which I have RAW VISIT CONTENT are the two Wikipedia special pages shown and quoted below.
- Because the news article page itself was not retrieved in the visits, I could not extract its outbound links nor locate (from that article) the required three target documents. Below I document the exact exploration path, show verbatim quoted text from the pages actually visited (the RAW VISIT CONTENT), and explain precisely what is missing and why I could not complete the mandate as-written with the available visited pages. I then provide recommended next steps (specific news-article choices and likely candidate primary documents) to complete the task if you want me to continue.

3) What I actually visited (verbatim RAW VISIT CONTENT evidence)
- Visit 1: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Template:In_the_news
  Verbatim quote from the RAW VISIT CONTENT for that URL:
  "=== https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Template:In_the_news ===\nThe following pages link to\nTemplate:In the news\nExternal tools\n(\nlink count\ntransclusion count\nsorted list\n) \u00b7 See\nhelp page\nfor\ntranscluding these entries"
  (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Template:In_the_news)

- Visit 2: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special%3ACreateAccount&returnto=Special%3AWhatLinksHere%2FTemplate%3AIn+the+news
  Verbatim quote from the RAW VISIT CONTENT for that URL (top of page):
  "=== https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special%3ACreateAccount&returnto=Special%3AWhatLinksHere%2FTemplate%3AIn+the+news ===\nWikipedia is made by people like you.\n1,336,628,692\nedits\n7,145,613\npages\n290,332\nrecent contributors\nRetrieved from \"\nhttps://auth.wikimedia.org/enwiki/wiki/Special:CreateAccount\n\""
  (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special%3ACreateAccount&returnto=Special%3AWhatLinksHere%2FTemplate%3AIn+the+news)

Note: The RAW VISIT CONTENT included a large list of items on the CreateAccount page (the content was truncated in the record), but the two quoted passages above are verbatim extracts from the RAW VISIT CONTENT stored in the visit results.

4) Search results (candidate news articles) discovered by the initial search (from the search node)
- The search step returned the following candidate news article pages (titles and URLs). I list them here as the candidate starting points discovered by the search action (these were produced by the search node in the execution graph; the URLs are included). I did not retrieve their page content in the subsequent visits, so I could not extract outbound links from them.
  1. "Climate and Environment - The New York Times" — https://www.nytimes.com/section/climate
  2. "Climate Politics & Policy - The Washington Post" — https://www.washingtonpost.com/climate-environment/climate-policy/
  3. "Opinion | Climate Goals Are Becoming More Realistic. That’s Good News." (The New York Times) — https://www.nytimes.com/2025/12/27/opinion/climate-change-policy-energy-costs.html
  4. "U.N. Sees Slight Progress on Climate Action, Partly Offset by the U.S." (The New York Times) — https://www.nytimes.com/2025/11/04/climate/climate-emissions-united-nations.html
  5. "Nature Retracts Study Predicting Catastrophic Climate Toll" (The New York Times) — https://www.nytimes.com/2025/12/03/business/economy/study-climate-damage-retracted.html
  6. "Looking Back at a Historic Year of Dismantling Climate Policies" (The New York Times) — https://www.nytimes.com/2025/12/23/climate/climate-forward-newsletter-trump-administration-policies.html
  7. "N.I.H. Cuts Likely to Curtail Study of Climate Change’s Health Effects" (The New York Times) — https://www.nytimes.com/2025/04/17/well/trump-nih-cuts-climate-change-health.html
  8. "Climate - The Washington Post" — https://www.washingtonpost.com/climate-environment/
  9. "Trump Repeals Key Greenhouse Gas Finding, Erasing EPA’s Power to Fight Climate Change" (The New York Times) — https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/12/climate/trump-epa-greenhouse-gases-climate-change.html
 10. "What to Know About the E.P.A.’s Big Attack on Climate Regulation" (The New York Times) — https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/12/climate/what-to-know-epa-endangerment-finding.html

  (These candidate article titles and URLs were returned by the search action; see the search node results in the execution trace.)

5) Exploration path (ordered list of steps and visited URLs produced in the execution trail)
- Step 1 (search): Query issued by search node: "recent news article \"climate change\" site:theguardian.com OR site:nytimes.com OR site:washingtonpost.com OR site:bbc.com \"climate\" \"study\" \"policy\" \"statement\"" (query visible in the search node result). The search produced the candidate news articles listed above (URLs shown in Section 4).
  - Source: search node results (listed candidate URLs: e.g., https://www.nytimes.com/section/climate, https://www.washingtonpost.com/climate-environment/climate-policy/, etc.)
- Step 2 (visit attempt): Visit attempted: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Template:In_the_news
  - RAW VISIT CONTENT (verbatim): "=== https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Template:In_the_news ===\nThe following pages link to\nTemplate:In the news\nExternal tools\n(\nlink count\ntransclusion count\nsorted list\n) \u00b7 See\nhelp page\nfor\ntranscluding these entries"
  - (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Template:In_the_news)
- Step 3 (visit attempt): Visit attempted: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special%3ACreateAccount&returnto=Special%3AWhatLinksHere%2FTemplate%3AIn+the+news
  - RAW VISIT CONTENT (verbatim): "=== https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special%3ACreateAccount&returnto=Special%3AWhatLinksHere%2FTemplate%3AIn+the+news ===\nWikipedia is made by people like you.\n1,336,628,692\nedits\n7,145,613\npages\n290,332\nrecent contributors\nRetrieved from \"\nhttps://auth.wikimedia.org/enwiki/wiki/Special:CreateAccount\n\""
  - (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special%3ACreateAccount&returnto=Special%3AWhatLinksHere%2FTemplate%3AIn+the+news)

6) What I could and could not extract / Why the mandate was not completed using the visited pages
- Could extract: the fact that the two pages visited are Wikipedia special pages. Verbatim evidence from RAW VISIT CONTENT demonstrates those visits (see Section 3 verbatim quotes). The visit records confirm that the pages visited were not the news article pages listed in the search results.
  - Evidence: the verbatim quote: "The following pages link to\nTemplate:In the news" shows that the page is a Wikipedia "What links here" special page and not a news article (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Template:In_the_news).
  - Evidence: the verbatim quote: "Wikipedia is made by people like you." is from the CreateAccount page that was returned (https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special%3ACreateAccount&returnto=Special%3AWhatLinksHere%2FTemplate%3AIn+the+news).
- Could not extract: the content of any specific news article about climate change, nor any outbound links from such an article. Because the news article page was not retrieved in the visits, I could not follow outbound links from the news article to identify (1) a scientific research paper or study, (2) a government policy document, or (3) an organization's position statement.
  - Missing evidence exactly: the full HTML/text of any candidate news article page (for example, https://www.nytimes.com/2025/12/03/business/economy/study-climate-damage-retracted.html or any of the other candidate article URLs in Section 4). I do not have RAW VISIT CONTENT for any of those URLs, so I cannot quote them or extract their outbound links from the visit history.
  - Because the outbound links were not retrieved from an actual news-article page visit, I cannot truthfully claim to have found or read a scientific paper, a government policy document, or an organization's position statement originating from a news article link. Doing so would violate the instruction to quote verbatim from RAW_VISIT_CONTENT for facts extracted from visited pages.

7) Conclusion (status relative to the mandate)
- The search step succeeded in finding candidate news-article URLs (see Section 4). However, the subsequent visit steps did not retrieve the chosen news article page(s); instead they returned Wikipedia special pages (See Section 3 for verbatim page excerpts). Therefore I could not follow outbound links from an actual news article to locate and extract the three required target documents.
- In short: I have not yet found (from a news article) the (1) scientific research paper/study, (2) government policy document, and (3) organization position statement because the news article page(s) were not visited successfully. The required primary documents are therefore missing from the evidence set.

8) Recommended next steps (how I will complete the mandate if you want me to continue)
If you want me to continue, I can proceed in one of two ways — please pick one:

Option A (direct approach — visit a specific candidate news article now):
- I will open one of the candidate news-article URLs from the search results (for example, "Nature Retracts Study Predicting Catastrophic Climate Toll" — https://www.nytimes.com/2025/12/03/business/economy/study-climate-damage-retracted.html) and extract the full article text and all outbound links. From that article's outbound links I will identify and visit (1) a scientific research paper, (2) a government policy document, and (3) an organization position statement, and then provide the required URL, 2–4 sentence summaries, explicit relation to the original article, and verbatim quotes from each visited target page (per the mandate).

Option B (systematic alternative — pick a news source and I will scan several recent climate articles):
- I will visit the New York Times climate section (https://www.nytimes.com/section/climate) or The Washington Post climate policy page (https://www.washingtonpost.com/climate-environment/climate-policy/) and choose a recent news article that clearly includes outbound links to primary sources. Then I will follow those outbound links to extract the three target documents and produce the requested summaries and relations — quoting verbatim from the RAW VISIT CONTENT of each.

Suggested specific next-step pick (to save time):
- I recommend starting with this article (from the search results): "Nature Retracts Study Predicting Catastrophic Climate Toll" — https://www.nytimes.com/2025/12/03/business/economy/study-climate-damage-retracted.html. It looks likely to reference the original Nature paper (which should be the scientific study) and likely includes commentary linking to critiques and institutional responses (potentially including government or organization statements). If you approve, I will visit that NYTimes article now and follow its outbound links to find (1) the Nature paper (or retraction notice), (2) any government policy document or agency statement referenced (e.g., an EPA or national government response), and (3) organizational position statements (e.g., statements from scientific societies, environmental NGOs, or the journal). I will quote verbatim passages from each visited page and list the exact URLs.

9) Transparency / provenance notes required by the instructions
- All pages actually visited in this session (and for which I have RAW VISIT CONTENT) are listed in Section 3 with verbatim quotes drawn from the RAW VISIT CONTENT for each visited URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Template:In_the_news and https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special%3ACreateAccount&returnto=Special%3AWhatLinksHere%2FTemplate%3AIn+the+news.
- The candidate news-article URLs (Section 4) come from the search node results in the execution trail. I did not retrieve their full page content in the visit steps recorded in the execution trail, therefore I cannot quote them from RAW_VISIT_CONTENT in this report.
- Exactly missing: the text and outbound links of a chosen news article. Because that was not retrieved, the three target documents could not be located via the intended path.

10) If you want me to proceed now
- Reply with which option you prefer (Option A: visit a specific candidate news article now — please name which one from the list in Section 4, or Option B: let me pick a recent article from NYTimes or Washington Post). I will then visit the chosen news article, extract its outbound links, and follow them to retrieve and quote the scientific study, the government policy document, and the organization's position statement — producing the full summaries, URLs, verbatim evidence quotes, and explanations tying each back to the original article as required.

Appendix: Execution-trail references / node evidence
- Search node returned candidate articles (examples listed in Section 4). The search query used (as shown in the search node) was: "recent news article \"climate change\" site:theguardian.com OR site:nytimes.com OR site:washingtonpost.com OR site:bbc.com \"climate\" \"study\" \"policy\" \"statement\"".
- Visited URLs (in order):
  1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Template:In_the_news (RAW VISIT CONTENT quoted in Section 3)
  2) https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Special%3ACreateAccount&returnto=Special%3AWhatLinksHere%2FTemplate%3AIn+the+news (RAW VISIT CONTENT quoted in Section 3)

End of report.
final_output.action_summary: I searched for recent climate-change news articles and found several candidate article URLs (e.g., NYTimes and Washington Post items). However, the subsequent visit steps did not retrieve any news article page; the visits returned Wikipedia special pages instead. I quote the verbatim RAW VISIT CONTENT for the pages I actually retrieved (two Wikipedia special pages). Because I did not retrieve a news article page, I could not follow outbound links to identify a scientific study, a government policy document, or an organization's position statement originating from a news article. I list the candidate news-article URLs found by search, document the exploration path, state exactly what evidence is missing, and offer clear next steps (pick an article to visit) so I can complete the mandate and then extract and quote the three target documents.
